prompt:
Write a complete aya Rust user-space program that loads an eBPF object file at path '/tmp/ebpf.o', attaches an XDP program named 'xdp_pass' to the network interface 'lo', and waits for Ctrl+C to detach.

Use aya 0.13 user-space API. Key imports:
use aya::Ebpf;  // NOT Bpf (deprecated)
use aya::programs::Xdp;  // or KProbe, TracePoint etc.
use aya::maps::{{HashMap, RingBuf, PerfEventArray}};
Include #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<(), anyhow::Error>.

Write a complete aya Rust user-space program that loads an eBPF object, reads all entries from a BPF HashMap named CONNECTIONS (key: u32, value: u64), and prints each key-value pair to stdout.

Use aya 0.13 user-space API. Key imports:
use aya::Ebpf;  // NOT Bpf (deprecated)
use aya::programs::Xdp;  // or KProbe, TracePoint etc.
use aya::maps::{{HashMap, RingBuf, PerfEventArray}};
Include #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<(), anyhow::Error>.

Write a complete async aya Rust user-space program using tokio that loads an eBPF ring buffer map named EVENTS and continuously reads events, printing each event's bytes as hex to stdout.

Use aya 0.13 user-space API. Key imports:
use aya::Ebpf;  // NOT Bpf (deprecated)
use aya::programs::Xdp;  // or KProbe, TracePoint etc.
use aya::maps::{{HashMap, RingBuf, PerfEventArray}};
Include #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<(), anyhow::Error>.

Write a complete aya Rust user-space program that attaches a kprobe eBPF program to the 'do_sys_open' kernel function, sets up a perf event array to receive events, and prints filename strings received from the eBPF program.

Use aya 0.13 user-space API. Key imports:
use aya::Ebpf;  // NOT Bpf (deprecated)
use aya::programs::Xdp;  // or KProbe, TracePoint etc.
use aya::maps::{{HashMap, RingBuf, PerfEventArray}};
Include #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<(), anyhow::Error>.

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that opens a pinned BPF map at path '/sys/fs/bpf/packet_count' (array map, key uint32, value uint64) and reads the value at key 0, printing it to stdout.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/rlimit"   // rlimit.RemoveMemlock()
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/ringbuf"  // ringbuf.NewReader
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/perf"     // perf.NewReader
Do NOT use github.com/cilium/ebpf/bpf — it does not exist.

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that loads a BPF object from '/tmp/counter.o', finds the map named 'packet_count', updates key uint32(0) with value uint64(100), and verifies the write by reading it back.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/rlimit"   // rlimit.RemoveMemlock()
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/ringbuf"  // ringbuf.NewReader
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/perf"     // perf.NewReader
Do NOT use github.com/cilium/ebpf/bpf — it does not exist.

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that loads a BPF object from '/tmp/xdp.o', attaches the XDP program named 'xdp_prog' to interface index 1 using XDP generic mode, and blocks until SIGINT is received before cleaning up.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe

Do NOT use github.com/cilium/ebpf/bpf — it does not exist.

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that iterates over all entries in a BPF hash map loaded from '/tmp/prog.o' with key type uint32 and value type uint64, printing each pair.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that loads a BPF object, attaches a kprobe to 'sys_execve', reads events from a perf event array named 'events', and prints raw event bytes.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that loads a BPF object from '/tmp/prog.o', pins the map named 'connections' to '/sys/fs/bpf/connections', and confirms the pin was successful.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe

Do NOT use github.com/cilium/ebpf/bpf — it does not exist.

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that uses the ring buffer reader to receive and print variable-length events from a BPF ring buffer map named 'rb', handling both lost samples and received records.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that loads a BPF object, attaches a TC classifier to the ingress of interface 'eth0' using netlink, and cleans up properly on exit.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/rlimit"   // rlimit.RemoveMemlock()
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/ringbuf"  // ringbuf.NewReader

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that loads a BPF object with BTF information, creates an LPM trie map with IPv4 prefix keys (struct with prefixlen uint32 and data [4]byte) and uint64 values, inserts a /24 prefix, and looks it up.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/rlimit"   // rlimit.RemoveMemlock()
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/ringbuf"  // ringbuf.NewReader
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/perf"     // perf.NewReader
Do NOT use github.com/cilium/ebpf/bpf — it does not exist.

Write a complete Go program using cilium/ebpf that implements a map-in-map (BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY_OF_MAPS) where each inner map is a hash map. Load it from a BPF object and look up an inner map by outer key 0.

Use cilium/ebpf v0.17 API. Key imports:
"github.com/cilium/ebpf"         // ebpf.LoadCollectionSpec, ebpf.Map
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/link"     // link.AttachXDP, link.AttachTracepoint, link.Kprobe
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/rlimit"   // rlimit.RemoveMemlock()
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/ringbuf"  // ringbuf.NewReader
"github.com/cilium/ebpf/perf"     // perf.NewReader
Do NOT use github.com/cilium/ebpf/bpf — it does not exist.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF kernel program using libbpf macros that defines a BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY map named 'pkt_count' with max_entries 1, and an XDP program that increments the counter at index 0 for every packet and returns XDP_PASS.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF kernel program using libbpf/BPF_PROG_TYPE_TRACEPOINT that attaches to the raw_tracepoint sys_enter and logs the syscall number using bpf_printk.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF kernel program that defines a BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH map named 'exec_count' keyed by pid_t with u64 values, and a kprobe on 'do_sys_openat2' that increments the count for the current PID using bpf_get_current_pid_tgid().

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF kernel program using CO-RE that defines a struct event { u32 pid; char comm[16]; } and a BPF_MAP_TYPE_RINGBUF map named 'rb', then a tracepoint on sched:sched_process_exec that submits an event with the current PID and comm.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF kernel XDP program that parses Ethernet, IPv4 and TCP headers, and blocks all packets destined for TCP port 8080, returning XDP_DROP. All other packets should be returned with XDP_PASS.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF TC classifier program that runs on egress, reads the destination IP from the IPv4 header, and records it in a BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH map named 'dst_ips' with the IP as key and packet count as value.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF kernel program using BPF_MAP_TYPE_LPM_TRIE to implement IP allowlist matching. The map key should be struct { u32 prefixlen; u32 addr; } and the value u8. Write an XDP program that drops packets if the source IP does NOT match any entry in the trie.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF kernel program using BPF_MAP_TYPE_SOCKMAP that implements socket redirection. Define the sockmap and a stream_verdict program that redirects all data on socket 0 to socket 1 using bpf_sk_redirect_map.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF kernel program that uses BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERCPU_HASH to track per-CPU packet counts keyed by source IPv4 address (u32). Write an XDP program that updates the per-CPU count for each packet's source IP.

Use CO-RE style with #include "vmlinux.h" and #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> only (do NOT include linux/ kernel headers directly). Write a complete C eBPF cgroup/sock program that blocks outbound connections to port 443 (HTTPS) by attaching to BPF_CGROUP_INET4_CONNECT and returning 0 to deny when the destination port is 443.

Explain the difference between BPF_MAP_TYPE_RINGBUF and BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERF_EVENT_ARRAY. When should you use each one? Include the key advantages of ring buffer over perf event array introduced in Linux 5.8.
Explain BTF (BPF Type Format) and CO-RE (Compile Once Run Everywhere). How do they work together to allow a single eBPF binary to run across different kernel versions without recompilation?
Explain the role of the eBPF verifier. What are three common reasons an eBPF program gets rejected by the verifier, and how do you fix each one?
What is the difference between a kprobe and a tracepoint in eBPF? Which is more stable across kernel versions and why? Give a specific example of when you would choose one over the other.
Explain BPF tail calls and BPF-to-BPF function calls. What are the key differences, limitations, and when would you use each? Include the stack depth implications of each approach.

Describe the complete lifecycle of an eBPF program from source code to kernel execution. Cover: compilation, loading via bpf() syscall, verification, JIT compilation, attachment, and map sharing between kernel and user space.
